topic: How to Build an AI Agent from Scratch 2026: Python + LangChain + Tools
keyword: how to build an AI agent 2026

competitors.title: How to Build an AI Agent with LangGraph Python in 14 Steps [2026]
key_points: LangGraph 1.1.6 with 126,000+ GitHub stars is the go-to framework for production-grade AI agents
Graph-based architecture: directed graphs with nodes (computation steps) and edges (control flow) supporting cycles, branching, conditional logic, persistent state

14-step tutorial: install → state schema → tools → graph nodes → conditional edges → streaming → checkpointing → human-in-the-loop → multi-agent → testing → deploy → monitoring → parallel execution → error recovery
State management: TypedDict with Annotated reducers (add_messages for conversation history), native checkpointing (SQLite/PostgreSQL)

Framework comparison table: LangGraph (directed graph, built-in checkpointing, moderate learning curve) vs CrewAI (role-based crews, low learning curve) vs AutoGen (conversation-based, limited checkpointing)
Cost estimate: under $2.00 in API calls for all tutorial steps
Hardware: 4GB RAM for cloud API mode, 16GB RAM + 8GB VRAM GPU for local Ollama models
LangGraph 1.1.x dropped Python 3.9 support, added Python 3.14 compatibility
LangGraph Platform for deployment, LangSmith for observability/monitoring
10 common troubleshooting issues with solutions (import errors, infinite loops, database locks, streaming issues)

competitors.title: How to Build AI Agents with LangChain: Complete 2026 Tutorial
key_points: Production-ready agent pattern: web search (Tavily) + database query + conversation memory + error handling + deployment
Uses LangChain AgentExecutor with create_react_agent and ReAct prompt pattern
Custom tools: @tool decorator for database queries and metrics calculations
Memory: ConversationBufferWindowMemory with SQLChatMessageHistory for persistent conversations
Evaluation: LangSmith integration for agent tracing and benchmarking
Deployment: FastAPI wrapping with Docker containerization
Python 3.10+, 30 minutes estimated build time
Error handling: handle_parsing_errors=True, max_iterations=5 in AgentExecutor

competitors.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Building an AI Agent with Python
key_points: Different approach: reinforcement learning (Q-Learning) with OpenAI Gym instead of LLM-based agents
Docker-based development environment (python:3.11-slim container)
Covers the perception-decision-action cycle fundamental to all AI agents
Q-Learning implementation with NumPy for numerical computations
Hyperparameter optimization for agent performance tuning
Docker containerization for deployment
11 min read, covers foundational concepts that complement LLM-based agent tutorials

competitors.title: 65 AI Agent Statistics 2026: Market Growth, ROI & Industry Trends
key_points: Global AI agents market: $7.63B in 2025 → $182.97B by 2033 (49.6% CAGR)
88% of organizations use AI in at least one function, only 6% are true AI high performers
62% of organizations are at least experimenting with AI agents (23% actively scaling, 39% experimenting)
61% of CEOs globally actively adopting AI agents and preparing to implement at scale
40% of enterprise apps will integrate task-specific AI agents by end of 2026 (up from <5% today)
25% of enterprises using GenAI expected to deploy AI agents by 2025, growing to 50% by 2027
North America holds 39.63% of global AI agents market revenue in 2025
By 2028, AI agents will intermediate more than $15 trillion in B2B spending
